microsoft_ft: Owned end-to-end customer engagement for **45+ annual resilience drills**, scaling Azure's recovery validation from a 2-person operation into a platformized system driving $14M+ impact across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience testing capability, delivering a **94% recovery rate** in 4 months by surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a continuous validation model adopted across enterprise deployment environments.

Served as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud **network isolation test** tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, directing 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility and aligning product, engineering, and operations to mission-critical deployment requirements.

Burned through organizational blockers across 140+ stakeholder teams to align cross-functional resources on customer objectives, mirroring the boots-on-ground execution model required for federal solution deployments at high ownership and high stakes.

Built and deployed an internal **AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, reducing planning cycle time by 39% and increasing drill capacity by 21% — freeing engineering bandwidth for higher-priority integration and rollout work.

Delivered executive-level presentations and written briefings to senior leadership on resilience program outcomes, translating complex technical results into strategic recommendations that shaped Azure's enterprise security and recovery roadmap.

Shaped the product roadmap toward **intent-based YAML generation** by conducting 11+ structured user interviews with Azure engineering teams, surfacing critical feature gaps and influencing prioritization to align product direction with on-the-ground operational needs.

Improved solution deployment speed by migrating documentation to an AI-powered **semantic search platform**, enforcing metadata standards that cut technical lookup time by 83% and accelerated troubleshooting for customer-facing engineering workflows.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework, cutting through coordination friction across 140+ teams to clear the path to successful execution.

Conducted discovery with 20+ service teams to surface 81 hours of manual toil per region launch, then built a phased **automation roadmap** targeting critical deployment paths — directly mirroring the integration and rollout ownership required in federal SA engagements.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to track operational toil and automation gaps across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to make data-driven prioritization decisions and allocate resources toward the highest-impact customer deployment bottlenecks.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** across a 2,000+ unit pilot by owning the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and serving as the on-site technical point of contact coordinating IT, Operations, and Engineering in real time.

Facilitated Agile sprint ceremonies and backlog prioritization to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets**, stabilizing the developer intake process and unblocking cross-functional teams from deployment delays — demonstrating the gritty, hands-on execution Cape's SA role requires.

Drove cross-functional alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and establishing a repeatable integration model for future large-scale rollouts.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end customer engagement lifecycle — building a new CRM workflow that managed 200+ monthly proposals and served as the single point of contact between field operations and sales leadership.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and lifted conversion rates by 2.7% by executing a data-driven go-to-market strategy, optimizing digital presence across the website and Google profile to target high-value customer segments more precisely.

Delivered financial profitability analysis across **1,000+ project records**, identifying margin leaks and remodeling leadership pricing frameworks — demonstrating the data-driven, analytic reasoning Cape requires when evaluating customer deployment trade-offs.

'product direction aligns with customers' on-the-ground reality' (JD competency) reflected in microsoft_2023 bullet#2, framing user interviews as alignment between roadmap and field-level needs.

'cut through bureaucratic obstacles' (JD bonus criterion) addressed directly in microsoft_2022 bullet#1, using 'cutting through coordination friction' and 'clear the path to successful execution' language pulled from the JD.
